title: Week 02 - Conditions and Loops
description: Practice conditional checks, loop counting, variable updates, and boundary checks.

questions.title: Check Whether an Exam Score Reaches the Passing Line

description.background: After grading an exam, a teacher needs to quickly check whether a student's score reaches the passing line. The school rule says that a score of 60 or above is passing, while a score below 60 is not passing.
description.task: Use the input exam score to decide whether this student passes, then return the result.
description.output: Return true when score reaches the passing line; otherwise return false.
description.hint: Check whether the score reaches the passing line first, then return the matching result.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(score)
    if score >= 60 then
        return true
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Check Whether a Student Number Is Odd or Even

description.background: In PE class, the teacher wants students to line up in two columns by number. Students with odd numbers stand in one column, and students with even numbers stand in the other column.
description.task: Determine whether the input student number is odd or even, then return the corresponding English result.
description.output: Return "odd" for an odd number and "even" for an even number.
description.hint: You can observe the remainder after dividing the number by 2.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(number)
    if number % 2 == 0 then
        return "even"
    end
    return "odd"
end

questions.title: Use a Loop to Count All Numbers from 1 to n

description.background: The teacher asks students to count numbers in order starting from 1 and continuing to n. To check whether the loop is correct, the final number counted needs to be known.
description.task: Use a loop to count from 1 to n, then return the last number counted.
description.output: Return the last number counted when the loop ends.
description.hint: You can use a variable to record the current number being counted.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local current = 0
    for i = 1, n do
        current = i
    end
    return current
end

questions.title: Check Whether a Game Character Is Still Alive After an Attack

description.background: A game character starts with hp health and loses damage health after one attack. The system needs to decide whether the character is still alive from the health after the attack.
description.task: First calculate the character's remaining health after the attack, then decide whether the character is still alive.
description.output: Return true if health after the attack is greater than 0; otherwise return false.
description.hint: Update the health first, then check the updated state.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(hp, damage)
    local remainHp = hp - damage
    if remainHp > 0 then
        return true
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Calculate How Many Rounds Until a Monster Is Defeated

description.background: A player is fighting a monster. The monster starts with hp health, and the player deals damage each round. As long as the monster's health is greater than 0, the battle continues.
description.task: Calculate how many attack rounds the player needs to defeat the monster.
description.output: Return the number of rounds needed to defeat the monster.
description.hint: Update the monster's health each round and record how many rounds have been played.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(hp, damage)
    local currentHp = hp
    local rounds = 0

    while currentHp > 0 do
        currentHp = currentHp - damage
        rounds = rounds + 1
    end

    return rounds
end

questions.title: Check Whether an Eraser Can Still Be Bought After Shopping

description.background: A student brings money to a stationery store and first buys count pencils, each costing pencilPrice. After buying pencils, the student wants to know whether the remaining money is enough to buy one eraser that costs eraserPrice.
description.task: Calculate the remaining money after buying pencils, then decide whether an eraser can still be bought.
description.input: Four numbers: money, pencilPrice, count, and eraserPrice.
description.output: Return true if the remaining money is enough to buy the eraser; otherwise return false.
description.hint: Calculate the money already spent first, then use the remaining money for the check.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(money, pencilPrice, count, eraserPrice)
    local cost = pencilPrice * count
    local leftMoney = money - cost

    if leftMoney >= eraserPrice then
        return true
    end
    return false
end

questions.title: Rating Settlement After Consecutive Game Battles

description.background: A game character goes through rounds consecutive battle rounds. The character starts with hp health. At the start of each round, the monster deals damage. If the round number is a multiple of 3, the character also takes trapDamage trap damage; if the round number is even, the character restores heal health at the end of that round. After battle ends, the system rates the character by final health.
description.task: Simulate the full battle process, then return the rating based on final health.
description.input: Five numbers: hp, damage, trapDamage, heal, and rounds.
description.output: Return "great" if final health is at least 80; return "survive" if final health is greater than 0 but less than 80; return "defeated" if final health is 0 or less.
description.hint: Split each round into normal damage, special damage, healing, and final rating steps.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(hp, damage, trapDamage, heal, rounds)
    local currentHp = hp

    for i = 1, rounds do
        currentHp = currentHp - damage

        if i % 3 == 0 then
            currentHp = currentHp - trapDamage
        end

        if i % 2 == 0 then
            currentHp = currentHp + heal
        end
    end

    if currentHp >= 80 then
        return "great"
    elseif currentHp > 0 then
        return "survive"
    end

    return "defeated"
end

questions.title: Final Result After Consecutive Vending Machine Purchases

description.background: A student inserts money into a vending machine. Each drink costs price. The student wants to buy at most want drinks, but the machine sells one drink at a time until there is not enough money or the desired count has been bought. The system then needs to classify the purchase result.
description.task: Simulate the consecutive purchase process and return the final purchase status.
description.input: Three numbers: money, price, and want.
description.output: Return "exact" if want drinks were bought and all money was spent; return "enough" if want drinks were bought with money left; return "partial" if fewer than want drinks were bought but at least one was bought; return "none" if no drink can be bought.
description.hint: Use a loop to simulate each purchase, then classify by the number bought and the remaining money.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(money, price, want)
    local leftMoney = money
    local bought = 0

    while leftMoney >= price and bought < want do
        leftMoney = leftMoney - price
        bought = bought + 1
    end

    if bought == want and leftMoney == 0 then
        return "exact"
    elseif bought == want then
        return "enough"
    elseif bought > 0 then
        return "partial"
    end

    return "none"
end

questions.title: Star Rating After Consecutive Game Level Scores

description.background: A player completes levels small levels in a row. Each level has baseScore base points. If the level number is even, the player gains evenBonus extra points; if the level number is a multiple of 5, the player gains hiddenBonus more points from a hidden task. After all levels end, the system gives a star rating by total score and average score.
description.task: Calculate the player's total score and average score, then return the final star rating.
description.input: Four numbers: levels, baseScore, evenBonus, and hiddenBonus.
description.output: Return 3 if the average is at least 90 and the total is at least 300; return 2 if the average is at least 70; return 1 if the total is greater than 0; otherwise return 0.
description.hint: Accumulate each level's score in the loop, then judge the star rating from total and average after the loop.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(levels, baseScore, evenBonus, hiddenBonus)
    local total = 0

    for i = 1, levels do
        local score = baseScore

        if i % 2 == 0 then
            score = score + evenBonus
        end

        if i % 5 == 0 then
            score = score + hiddenBonus
        end

        total = total + score
    end

    local average = 0
    if levels > 0 then
        average = total / levels
    end

    if average >= 90 and total >= 300 then
        return 3
    elseif average >= 70 then
        return 2
    elseif total > 0 then
        return 1
    end

    return 0
end
